architecture.summary: LLM-based agents operate in a multi-agent cooperative text game with Theory of Mind inference requirements. Agents receive text observations of game state and must coordinate with other agents to complete tasks. The system compares three agent types: LLM-based (which read task state as text and generate actions), MARL baselines (reinforcement learning), and planning-based baselines. A key intervention adds explicit belief state representations (text descriptions of agent beliefs about other agents' knowledge) that are injected into the LLM context to improve planning and ToM reasoning.

biblio.title: Theory of Mind for Multi-Agent Collaboration via Large Language Models

claims.statement: LLM-based agents demonstrate evidence of emergent collaborative behaviors and high-order Theory of Mind capabilities
LLM-based agents show systematic failures in managing long-horizon contexts and hallucination about task state

baseline.name: LLM-based agents without belief state representations

claims.statement: Explicit belief state representations enhance task performance and accuracy of ToM inferences for LLM-based agents
LLM-based agents show limitations in planning optimization due to long-horizon context management failures
